lines: +// AI Engine — Board Evaluation & Minimax Search
+// Piece-square tables (from white's perspective; mirrored for black)
+// Values are added to the base piece value for positional scoring.
+  // White pieces use the table as-is; black pieces mirror vertically
+      const positional = getPstValue(p.type, p.color, r, c);
+// Generate all legal moves for a given color in the current boardState.
+// Each move is { fromRow, fromCol, toRow, toCol, enPassant?, castling? }
+        moves.push({ fromRow: r, fromCol: c, toRow: m.row, toCol: m.col, enPassant: m.enPassant, castling: m.castling });
+// Apply a move on boardState, returning an undo object to restore later.
+// Handles captures, en passant, castling, and auto-queen promotion.
+  const { fromRow, fromCol, toRow, toCol } = move;
+  // Pawn promotion — always promote to queen for AI
+  // Update lastMove for en passant detection in deeper searches
+  lastMove = { piece: { ...piece }, fromRow, fromCol, toRow, toCol };
+  const { fromRow, fromCol, toRow, toCol } = undo;
+  // Restore the piece (use original if it was promoted)
+// Returns the evaluation score from the perspective of the maximising player (white).
+// isMaximising: true when it's white's turn to move
+  // No legal moves: checkmate or stalemate
+      // Checkmate — very bad for the side to move
+// Returns the best move for the current player at the given search depth.
+// Returns { fromRow, fromCol, toRow, toCol, enPassant?, castling? } or null if no moves.

input.command:
git add index.html && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add AI engine with board evaluation and minimax search

Implement chess AI with material evaluation (pawn=100, knight=320,
bishop=330, rook=500, queen=900, king=20000), piece-square tables for
positional scoring, and minimax search algorithm. The getBestMove(depth)
function returns the best move for the current player, handling all
special moves (castling, en passant, auto-queen promotion) and avoiding
illegal moves that leave the king in check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --draft --title "Add AI engine with board evaluation and minimax search" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Add material evaluation function with standard piece values (pawn=100, knight=320, bishop=330, rook=500, queen=900, king=20000)
- Add piece-square tables for positional scoring (center control, development, king safety)
- Implement minimax search algorithm with configurable depth via `getBestMove(depth)`
- AI correctly handles all special moves: castling, en passant, and pawn promotion (always promotes to queen)

## Test plan
- [ ] Open `index.html` in a browser and call `getBestMove(3)` from the console — verify it returns a valid move object
- [ ] Verify the returned move is legal (does not leave own king in check)
- [ ] Test at depth 3 completes in under 3 seconds on a modern browser
- [ ] Verify AI handles en passant scenarios correctly
- [ ] Verify AI handles castling moves correctly
- [ ] Verify AI auto-promotes pawns to queen

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
All done. The only uncommitted change is `.claude-hub.jsonl` which is unrelated to this task.

**PR created**: https://github.com/minyic7/claude-hub-demo-chess/pull/7

Here's what was added to `index.html`:

- **`AI_PIECE_VALUES`** — material values (pawn=100, knight=320, bishop=330, rook=500, queen=900, king=20000)
- **`PST`** — piece-square tables for all 6 piece types (center control, development, king safety)
- **`evaluateBoard()`** — scores the position by summing material + positional values for both sides
- **`generateAllLegalMoves(color)`** — collects all legal moves for a side
- **`applyMove(move)` / `undoMove(undo)`** — make/unmake moves on the board for search, handling castling, en passant, and auto-queen promotion
- **`minimax(depth, isMaximising)`** — standard minimax without alpha-beta pruning
- **`getBestMove(depth)`** — entry point that returns the best move for `currentTurn`

result:
All done. The only uncommitted change is `.claude-hub.jsonl` which is unrelated to this task.

**PR created**: https://github.com/minyic7/claude-hub-demo-chess/pull/7

Here's what was added to `index.html`:

- **`AI_PIECE_VALUES`** — material values (pawn=100, knight=320, bishop=330, rook=500, queen=900, king=20000)
- **`PST`** — piece-square tables for all 6 piece types (center control, development, king safety)
- **`evaluateBoard()`** — scores the position by summing material + positional values for both sides
- **`generateAllLegalMoves(color)`** — collects all legal moves for a side
- **`applyMove(move)` / `undoMove(undo)`** — make/unmake moves on the board for search, handling castling, en passant, and auto-queen promotion
- **`minimax(depth, isMaximising)`** — standard minimax without alpha-beta pruning
- **`getBestMove(depth)`** — entry point that returns the best move for `currentTurn`
